metadata.challenge_summary: Different subtasks favor different heroes: scouting reflection lines, disarming vault wards, and carrying the blade token.
dilemmas: The elf sees routes quickly but cannot safely handle the trap
The dwarf can open the vault but is slower
Carrying the blade may expose the scout
warden_runbook: Avoid making one role mandatory for basic success
metadata.lite_focus: A compact vault where one hero scouts reflection, the other handles hazard, and the token may need a handoff.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: assign scout, disarm, and handoff roles before taking the vault item
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: pressure the declared MARL axis: heterogeneous role-task assignment
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: coordinate extraction once the objective is secured
coordination_skills: wait for the specialist before opening vault hazards
announce optional loot before taking it
protect the key or blade carrier on exit
metadata.tier_test_focus: Small two-hero diagnostic for communication and first-order specialist sequencing and greed control coordination.
coordination_demands: wait for the specialist before opening vault hazards
announce optional loot before taking it
protect the key or blade carrier on exit

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into Moonblade Vault is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

middle_room.name: Middle Room
middle_room.description: A compact pressure room isolates the challenge: heterogeneous role-task assignment.

objective_room.name: Objective Room
objective_room.description: The core object of the lite challenge is visible here; taking it should trigger extraction thinking, not exhaustive exploration.

lower_route.name: Lower Route
lower_route.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Moon-Slit Mirror
furniture.description: Primary clue object for heterogeneous role-task assignment. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains heterogeneous role-task assignment.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: A compact vault where one hero scouts reflection, the other handles hazard, and the token may need a handoff.

core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: pressure the declared MARL axis: heterogeneous role-task assignment
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.

iron_sentinel_1.name: Moon-Vault Sentinel
iron_sentinel_1.public_summary: Lite boss for heterogeneous role-task assignment; it should test coordination before raw damage.
iron_sentinel_1.counterplay_hint: Search the focus object and coordinate roles before committing to damage.
iron_sentinel_1.defeat_description: Defeat Moon-Vault Sentinel after using the lite challenge's coordination lesson.
